title: thymus maroccanus var. lythroides
intro: Thymus maroccanus var. lythroides, also known as Moroccan Thyme, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Thymus genus. It is a low-growing, woody perennial herb with a strong fragrance, used for its medicinal and culinary properties. The plant's leaves and flowers have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and antimicrobial properties, which help to relieve congestion and soothe the throat. Herbal preparations of Moroccan Thyme include tea infusions, essential oil extracts, and dried leaf infusions, which can be used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
